_comment: Pre-Crime sample manifest — caricature artist, Los Angeles. Copy this and fill in your own details.

product.name: Party Caricatures & Coloring Party Entertainment
product.description: Live caricature and coloring party entertainment for LA-area events. Former Warner Bros./DC Comics artist. $175/hour caricatures (12-15 faces/hour), $200/hour coloring parties. Full setup provided — chair, lighting, supplies. Custom-branded event paper available.
differentiators: Former Warner Bros. / DC Comics artist — a legitimate pro credential that guests recognize
12-15 faces per hour — high-throughput quality work, no long guest lines
Complete self-contained setup — zero burden on event host, no rider requirements
Custom paper with event branding — guests take home a named keepsake, not a generic party favor
Accepts all payment types (cash, card, Venmo, PayPal, Zelle)
product.geography: Los Angeles metro — San Fernando Valley, Inland Empire, Pasadena, Burbank, Glendale, Long Beach, Santa Monica, Torrance
product.pricing: $175/hour caricatures, $200/hour coloring parties. One-hour deposit to hold date. Full payment on completion.

factletTopics: Corporate event planning trends (return-to-office, in-person events)
School event funding (budget cuts affecting ASB/activities)
Experiential marketing and live event trends
factletNotTopics: General business news unrelated to events or entertainment
Political content unless directly affecting event budgets

criteria.0: No contact found or no email
criteria.1: Generic inbox (info@, events@) or role unclear
criteria.2: Named event decision-maker (ASB advisor, HR manager, event chair) with direct email

criteria.1: Planning season is active (within 8-week lead window for their segment)
criteria.2: Specific upcoming event identified with date OR event explicitly announced publicly

criteria.1: Historical event evidence (past year recap, past Eventbrite, venue relationship)
criteria.2: Active planning signal (job posting for event help, announcement, registration open)

criteria.0: Page not found or no content
criteria.1: Found and scraped — recent activity visible
Scraped something useful (staff, events page, mission, venue capacity)
At least one specific fact (event name, date, attendance, past entertainment)

criteria.0: Nothing specific enough to open an email with
criteria.1: Clear season-specific or event-specific opener available

hardGates: No email at all → warmthScore = 0, draftStatus = brewing, do not compose
Client outside LA metro service area → warmthScore = 0, note as OUT_OF_GEOGRAPHY

evaluatorCriteria.name: Specificity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft reference their actual event or organization — not 'events like yours'?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: 'Your spring carnival is coming up in April...' or 'I saw you're planning your annual holiday party...'
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: 'Many schools love having caricature artists at their events...'

evaluatorCriteria.name: Timing
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft land during an active planning window? Is there booking urgency?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: 'Prom season books fast — most weekend dates in April and May are already claimed.'
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Email sent in July about prom. Email with no timing signal at all.

evaluatorCriteria.name: Value Bridge
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft connect their event need to Scott's specific offer in one sentence?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: '12-15 faces an hour, full setup, custom paper with your school crest — guests take home something.'
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: 'I'm a professional caricature artist available for hire.'

evaluatorCriteria.name: Brevity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Under 100 words. Every sentence earns its place. Tighter than a SaaS cold email.
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Over 100 words. Or contains any forbidden phrase.

evaluatorCriteria.name: Reply Test
evaluatorCriteria.description: Would a busy event planner forward this to their principal or boss?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: Earns a calendar check. They might forward it with 'thoughts?'
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Finger on delete by sentence two.

outreachRules.tone: casual, confident, concrete — sounds like a working professional, not a vendor
outreachRules.openWith: Their event name or season. Never Scott's name or credential as the first sentence.
outreachRules.closeWith: Booking urgency — 'Dates in [month] go fast — happy to hold one for you.'
forbidden: I hope this finds you well
I am a caricature artist [as opening sentence]
